Acacia loroloba is a plant species that belongs to the Acacia genus, a group of flowering plants in the legume family (Fabaceae). Acacia loroloba has been found to have various health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against infections and diseases.

The bioactive constituents of Acacia loroloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and condensed tannins, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
